task_context.description: This is a systematic electromagnetic shower characterization study using Monte Carlo simulation to understand how calorimeter performance scales with particle energy. The task involves simulating electron showers at multiple energies in a homogeneous PbWO4 crystal calorimeter and measuring key shower properties including longitudinal and transverse profiles, shower maximum position, containment radius, and energy resolution. The study aims to validate theoretical predictions about shower development scaling laws and determine practical design requirements for high-energy calorimetry.
task_context.goal: Characterize how electromagnetic shower dimensions and properties scale with energy to inform calorimeter design requirements for high-energy physics applications
constraints: Use tractable simulation parameters for computational feasibility
Detector must be large enough for full containment at highest energy
Limited to 3 energy points for practical simulation time
Must use homogeneous PbWO4 crystal without absorber layers

observations.content: Step completed successfully: Generate PbWO4 crystal calorimeter geometry with 25 X₀ depth for full shower containment at 100 GeV
Step completed successfully: Run electromagnetic shower simulations at 10, 50, and 100 GeV to characterize shower development scaling
Step completed successfully: Extract longitudinal and transverse shower profiles, determine shower maximum positions and containment radii
Step completed successfully: Determine 95% containment requirements and assess leakage at 25 X₀ depth for design optimization
Step completed successfully: Generate comprehensive report with calorimeter design requirements and scaling relationships for high-energy applications

methodology_notes: Planned 6-step workflow: generate_geometry_pbwo4_homogeneous_slab, simulate_energy_sweep_pbwo4_homogeneous_slab, analyze_shower_profiles_pbwo4, validate_scaling_laws, study_containment_requirements, generate_design_report
